mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth acts as a continuous trend driver for equity valuations. Sustained positive GDPC1 readings reduce earnings uncertainty and support forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation forces the Federal Reserve to hike rates aggressively, compressing equity multiples and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the inflation trigger activates, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of strong nonfarm payrolls growth and a stable unemployment rate signals a resilient labor market that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its historical trend indicates supply-side bottlenecks or weakening manufacturing demand, which negatively impacts corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correspond to statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns over the 5-day horizon, or the signal reverses direction during actual supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts serve as a continuous leading indicator for consumer confidence and construction-related economic activity, providing a persistent positive drift to S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST exhibits a negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.4 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered events, or the unemployment trigger fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of elevated inflation and rising short-term interest rates creates a dual pressure on discount rates and real yields, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative equity returns when both series move in the specified direction, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail and food services sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which negatively impacts corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines do not correspond to statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns over the 5-day horizon, or the signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
